commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can align doctor trusted-proxy proof with runtime local-interface rejection; maintainer security-boundary acceptance remains after repair.; Needs attention: The diff has a concrete security-diagnostic gap: one runtime-rejected trusted-proxy source can be classified as warning-level auth proof.; Review finding: [P2] Reject host-interface trusted proxies as proof — src/commands/doctor-... (sha=323929cb1b14b8718563ebff08d0427d1e277df9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `openclaw doctor` Gateway exposure diagnostics so complete trusted-proxy auth downgrades from unauthenticated critical output to trusted-proxy-specific warnings while adding regression tests for invalid proxy setups.

PR surface: Source +112, Tests +148. Total +260 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: configure `gateway.bind="lan"` with `gateway.auth.mode="trusted-proxy"`, `gateway.auth.trustedProxy.userHeader`, and `gateway.trustedProxies`; current main still follows the shared-secret-only unauthenticated doctor branch, while the PR's new proof misses runtime local-interface rejection.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can align doctor trusted-proxy proof with runtime local-interface rejection; maintainer security-boundary acceptance remains after repair.

target.title: fix: treat trusted-proxy gateway auth as doctor auth proof
target.body:
## Summary

- Count a complete `trusted-proxy` gateway setup as authentication proof for the network-exposure doctor check.
- Keep incomplete trusted-proxy setups on the existing critical path by requiring both `gateway.auth.trustedProxy.userHeader` and `gateway.trustedProxies`.
- Keep startup-invalid trusted-proxy/token conflicts and unsafe proxy source configs on specific critical paths.
- Require every non-empty `gateway.trustedProxies` entry to be a host-scoped trusted proxy source before doctor downgrades exposed Gateway auth from critical to warning.
- Replace the misleading token/`doctor --fix` remediation with trusted-proxy-specific proxy/firewall guidance.

## Why

`trusted-proxy` is an explicit gateway auth mode, and the gateway runtime already treats the proxy source boundary as security-sensitive. The doctor exposure check only recognized token/password shared-secret modes, so a LAN-bound trusted-proxy deployment could be reported as "without authentication" and nudged toward token repair even though the intended auth boundary is the reverse proxy.

This keeps the exposure warning, because a LAN-bound gateway still deserves review, but it no longer labels a complete trusted-proxy setup as unauthenticated.

It also avoids treating unsafe or startup-invalid trusted-proxy configs as proof: shared-token conflicts, missing user headers, missing proxy sources, broad proxy CIDRs/default routes, mixed exact-plus-broad proxy lists, and loopback proxy entries without explicit `allowLoopback` remain critical.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: LAN-bound Gateway configs using fully configured `trusted-proxy` auth were reported as unauthenticated by doctor/security.
- Real environment tested: A real local OpenClaw config using `gateway.bind=lan`, `gateway.auth.mode=trusted-proxy`, and configured trusted proxies.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `npm_config_cache=/private/tmp/openclaw-npm-cache npm exec --yes pnpm@11.2.2 -- node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json`
- Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the patched dev CLI included these `core/doctor/security` findings:

```json
[
  {
    "checkId": "core/doctor/security",
    "severity": "warning",
    "message": "WARNING: Gateway bound to \"lan\" (0.0.0.0) with trusted-proxy authentication."
  },
  {
    "checkId": "core/doctor/security",
    "severity": "warning",
    "message": "Ensure only configured trusted proxies can reach the Gateway port; block direct clients at a host firewall, network firewall, or loopback bind."
  },
  {
    "checkId": "core/doctor/security",
    "severity": "warning",
    "message": "Docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/trusted-proxy-auth"
  }
]
```

- Observed result after fix: The trusted-proxy Gateway config is no longer reported as `CRITICAL` or "without authentication"; doctor keeps a network-exposure warning with trusted-proxy-specific guidance.
- What was not tested: I did not test a live remote reverse-proxy login flow from this branch; the proof is limited to the doctor/security classification and remediation text.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-security.test.ts` - 40 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-security.ts src/commands/doctor-security.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `npm_config_cache=/private/tmp/openclaw-npm-cache npm exec --yes pnpm@11.2.2 -- lint`
target.author: veteranbv
